lede: A look at November 17 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Greg Rutherford and Louis XVIII, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Tatarstan Airlines Flight 363
events.desc: Fifty people are killed when Tatarstan Airlines Flight 363 crashes at Kazan Airport, Russia.

events.title: Tornado outbreak of November 17, 2013
events.desc: A rare late-season tornado outbreak strikes the Midwest. Illinois and Indiana are most affected with tornado reports as far north as lower Michigan. In all around six dozen tornadoes touch down in approximately an 11-hour time period, including seven EF3 and two EF4 tornadoes.

events.title: Arnold Schwarzenegger
events.desc: Actor Arnold Schwarzenegger's tenure as the governor of California begins.

events.title: BAC One-Eleven
events.desc: British European Airways introduces the BAC One-Eleven into commercial service.

events.title: 14th Dalai Lama
events.desc: Lhamo Dondrub is officially named the 14th Dalai Lama.

events.title: John Bardeen
events.desc: American scientists John Bardeen and Walter Houser Brattain observe the basic principles of the transistor, a key element for the electronics revolution of the 20th century.

events.title: Egypt
events.desc: In Egypt, the Suez Canal, linking the Mediterranean Sea with the Red Sea, is inaugurated.

events.title: Ecuador
events.desc: Ecuador and Venezuela are separated from Gran Colombia.

events.title: French Revolutionary Wars
events.desc: French Revolutionary Wars: Battle of the Bridge of Arcole: French forces defeat the Austrians in Italy.

events.title: John Balliol
events.desc: John Balliol becomes King of Scotland.

births.name: John Boehner
births.line: American businessman and politician, 53rd Speaker of the United States House of Representatives

births.name: Terry Branstad
births.line: American soldier, lawyer, and politician, 39th Governor of Iowa and U.S. Ambassador to China

deaths.text: Doris Lessing, British novelist, poet, playwright, Nobel Prize laureate
Mervyn Peake, English poet, author, and illustrator
John III of Sweden, King of Sweden from 1569 to 1592

observances.text: Christian feast days: Gennadius of Constantinople (Greek Orthodox Church)
Christian feast days: Gregory of Tours (Roman Catholic Church)
